mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for construction, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite HOUST rising by > 10% in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when PAYEMS > 100k and FEDFUNDS < 4% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market, which supports corporate pricing power and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns during periods when UNRATE < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite INDPRO rising by > 5% in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 202.0 signals central bank inaction, which erodes real returns and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns despite CPIAUCSL rising by > 0.5pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflation' risk, which depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive when CPIAUCSL > 202 and FEDFUNDS < 4.5% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite RSAFS rising by > 5% in 2 months.
